sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
I'm at 36 actions and still haven't solved the level. I've tried clicking on almost everything. Let me check what happened when I clicked on the dividers.
Let me also try clicking on the red dots and the gray bars, which I haven't tried yet (or at least not systematically).
Actually, let me first check what changed when I clicked on the dividers.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed when I clicked on the dividers
if last_transition:
    before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
    after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation
    before_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in before['nodes']}
    after_nodes = {n['id']: n for n in after['nodes']}
    
    print("Differences from last action (clicking on dividers):")
    all_ids = set(list(before_nodes.keys()) + list(after_nodes.keys()))
    for nid in sorted(all_ids):
        b = before_nodes.get(nid)
        a = after_nodes.get(nid)
        if b is None:
            print(f"  id={nid} APPEARED: color={a['color']} pix={a['pixels']}")
        elif a is None:
            print(f"  id={nid} DISAPPEARED: color={b['color']} pix={b['pixels']}")
        elif b['pixels'] != a['pixels'] or b['boundary'] != a['boundary']:
            print(f"  id={nid} CHANGED: pix {b['pixels']}->{a['pixels']} bound {b['boundary']}->{a['boundary']}")
    
    # Also check the green and yellow source sizes
    green = after_nodes.get(2, {}).get('pixels', '?')
    yellow = after_nodes.get(16, {}).get('pixels', '?')
    print(f"\nCurrent: green={green}, yellow={yellow}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

So the dividers are NOT interactive. The only interactive elements are the U-shapes and the dark gray inner regions, and they both change the source object sizes.
I've tried all 6 possible source object size combinations and none completed the level. This means the goal is NOT about the source object sizes.

- id=17: (31, 10) - yellow red dot
